results.rendered_html:
un-√mad (ud-√mad) P. -mādyati, to become disordered in intellect or distracted, be or become mad or furious,  TS. ;  TBr. ;  TāṇḍyaBr. ;  ŚBr. ;  MBh. ;  Kathās.  :
Caus. -madayati, or -mādayati, to excite, agitate,  AV. vi, 130, 4  (see also un-madita);
to make furious or drunk, inebriate, madden,  TS.  :  MBh. ;  R. ;  Daś.  (cf. un-mand, next page.)

results.rendered_html:
unmad 4 P.
1 To be or become mad.
2 To delight, gratify.
— Caus. (ma-mā-dayati) To madden, inebriate, render drunk (lit. and fig.); lakṣmyonmāditāḥ  K. 107 ;  Ki. 4. 16.
